# manager.trainer.load_iteration { #spotforecast2_safe.manager.trainer.load_iteration }

```python
manager.trainer.load_iteration(name, iteration, model_dir=None)
```

Load a saved model at a given iteration.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                                                                                             | Description                                                                       | Default    |
|-----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| name      | [str](`str`)                                                                                     | Model name (e.g. ``"lgbm"``).                                                     | _required_ |
| iteration | [int](`int`)                                                                                     | Iteration of the model.                                                           | _required_ |
| model_dir | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[Union](`typing.Union`)\[[str](`str`), [Path](`pathlib.Path`)\]\] | Directory where models are stored. If *None*, defaults to :func:`get_cache_home`. | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                 | Description                                                      |
|--------|------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[Any](`typing.Any`)\] | The loaded model instance, or *None* if the file does not exist. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import tempfile
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.manager.trainer import load_iteration
>>> with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
...     result = load_iteration("lgbm", 99, model_dir=tmpdir)
...     result is None
True
```
